seo.title: Kling 3 4K | Native 4K AI Video Model | MaxVideoAI
seo.description: Use Kling 3 4K on MaxVideoAI for native 4K text-to-video and image-to-video delivery renders with 3-15s clips, optional audio, and final master pricing.

overview: Kling 3 4K is the native 4K Kling AI video route for final delivery renders, approved text-to-video prompts, image-to-video masters, locked framing, and optional native audio.
pricingNotes: Member display estimates include the MaxVideoAI margin: about $0.55/s for native 4K output, so use this route for approved final renders.

hero.title: Kling 3 4K - native 4K video generation
hero.intro: Use Kling 3 4K when a prompt or start frame is already approved and the shot needs native 4K output for delivery, editing or large-screen review. Use Kling 3.0 Omni 4K when reference images or storyboard frames should guide the 4K shot without becoming the opening frame.
hero.badge: Native 4K · 3-15s · Text or image input · Audio on/off

items.title: Product hero shots and premium ads
Campaign masters for editing and cropping

technicalOverview.label: Details
technicalOverview.body: Kling 3 4K uses the active native 4K route, so it should be treated as a delivery route rather than a lower-cost ideation model.

technicalOverview.label: Inputs
technicalOverview.body: Text-to-video; image-to-video from one source image with optional end frame. No video input on this MaxVideoAI route.

technicalOverview.label: Audio
technicalOverview.body: Native audio can be toggled on or off; the active route currently shows the same 4K rate either way

promptStructure.title: Prompting Kling 3 4K
promptStructure.quote: Use the same approved single-prompt or multi-prompt recipe from Standard or Pro, then render the final version in native 4K.
promptStructure.description: Keep the shot plan tight and avoid using 4K for early prompt exploration.
steps: Validate composition in Standard or Pro
Move the approved single-prompt or multi-prompt plan to Kling 3 4K
Keep one clear action per shot
Use start frames for final framing

tips.title: Tips & limits
items: Use Kling 3 4K after the creative direction is already approved.
Native 4K is more expensive than Kling 3 Pro 1080p.
Use Standard or Pro for cheaper iteration.

prompts.title: Native 4K product reveal
prompts.prompt: Native 4K cinematic product reveal: macro reflections across a polished device, slow dolly push, controlled studio lighting, crisp edges, subtle ambient audio.

prompts.title: 4K travel campaign master
prompts.prompt: 16:9 native 4K travel hero shot: sunrise over a cliffside hotel, smooth drone glide, warm haze, ocean spray, refined luxury campaign feel.

faqs.a: Yes. Kling 3 4K is the native 4K delivery route on MaxVideoAI for selected text-to-video or image-to-video shots.

faqs.q: When should I use Kling 3 4K instead of Kling 3 Pro?
faqs.a: Use Kling 3 Pro for most creative validation and 1080p approvals. Use Kling 3 4K only when the approved shot needs native 4K final delivery.
Yes. The current MaxVideoAI route supports image-to-video from one source image, with an optional end frame where exposed by the active route.

faqs.q: Does Kling 3 4K support audio and lip sync?
faqs.a: Yes, audio can be toggled and lip sync is supported. Keep dialogue short and face framing stable when lip sync matters.

faqs.q: How long can Kling 3 4K clips be?
faqs.a: The route is short-form, with duration options up to 15 seconds. Confirm the final duration and quote in Generate before rendering.

custom.galleryIntro: Review approved Kling shots that deserve native 4K output: product masters, premium ads, large-screen edits, and high-resolution image-to-video approvals.
quickPricingItems: Draft in Kling 3 Standard when you need more variants at the lowest current Kling 3 rate.
Move stronger directions into Kling 3 Pro for the main 1080p quality pass.
Use Kling 3 4K only for approved delivery renders, where native 4K clarity is worth the higher per-second price.
custom.relatedSubtitle: Keep 4K as the delivery step in the Kling family instead of replacing the Pro workhorse.

relatedItems.title: Kling 3 Pro
relatedItems.description: Use Pro for the main creative pass, multi-shot control, and lower-cost 1080p approvals before final 4K export.

relatedItems.title: Kling 3 Standard
relatedItems.description: Use Standard for prompt testing and storyboard variants before spending on Pro or 4K.

custom.finalPara1: Kling 3 4K is strongest when the creative direction is already proven. Treat it as the native 4K master route for approved shots.
custom.finalPara2: For most teams, the efficient workflow is Standard for exploration, Pro for the primary approval pass, then 4K only for final delivery.
custom.promptingIntro: Kling 3 4K works best as a final delivery pass. Start from an approved prompt or start frame, lock framing and duration, decide whether audio belongs in the master, then render only the selected shot in native 4K.
custom.promptingTip: Tip: validate timing, motion and framing in Kling 3 Standard or Pro before spending on the 4K master.
promptingGlobalPrinciples: Use one approved shot brief instead of exploring multiple creative directions in 4K.
Lock subject, action, camera, aspect ratio and duration before rendering the final master.
Use a clean source image when product geometry, identity or framing must stay stable.
Decide audio on/off before export; treat audio as part of the delivery spec.
Control cost by drafting lower first, then moving only approved shots to Kling 3 4K.
promptingEngineWhy: Kling 3 4K is exposed as text-to-video and image-to-video on MaxVideoAI, not as a video-input route.
Image-to-video accepts one source image, with an optional end frame where the active route exposes it.
The route is native 4K, so it fits final masters better than broad prompt exploration.
Lip sync is supported, but the prompt still needs short readable dialogue and stable face framing.
Use the final quote in Generate before client-facing delivery or bulk rendering.

promptingTabs.label: Final master
promptingTabs.title: Final 4K delivery prompt
promptingTabs.description: Use this when the shot recipe is already approved.
promptingTabs.copy:
Template (copy / paste)
Shot goal: [final delivery use: product master, campaign clip, large-screen review]
Subject: [approved product / character / scene]
Action: [one clean readable action]
Camera: [locked framing + one camera move]
Style: [approved lighting, palette, lens feel]
Delivery: native 4K, [duration], [aspect ratio], audio [on/off]

Example:
Final native 4K product master for a premium smartwatch on a dark graphite plinth. One slow dolly push reveals the screen reflection and polished metal edge. Locked 16:9 framing, crisp studio highlights, black background, no extra text, no logo changes. Audio off for editorial finishing.

promptingTabs.label: Reference
promptingTabs.title: Image-to-video master prompt
promptingTabs.description: Use this when a source frame controls product identity or composition.
promptingTabs.copy:
Template (copy / paste)
Source image role: [identity / product geometry / final framing]
Keep: [shape, logo, color, pose, composition]
Animate: [single motion beat]
Camera: [small push / orbit / locked shot]
End frame: [optional landing frame if needed]
Delivery: native 4K, [duration], audio [on/off]

Example:
Use the approved product still as the source frame. Preserve bottle shape, label placement and amber glass color. Add a subtle 4K macro orbit with controlled reflections, keep the bottle centered, land on the same clean frontal composition, no added text. Audio on with soft studio ambience only.

promptingTabs.label: Audio
promptingTabs.title: Audio decision prompt
promptingTabs.description: Use this when the 4K delivery file needs sound context.
promptingTabs.copy:
Template (copy / paste)
Audio role: [review context / final ambience / short voice / no audio]
Visible action tied to sound: [what creates the cue]
Avoid: [long dialogue, competing music beds, noisy off-screen action]
Export note: [audio on/off]

Example:
Native 4K fashion campaign master, model turns once under soft studio light. Audio on: quiet fabric movement, subtle room tone, one low cinematic hit at the turn. No dialogue, no music bed, no off-screen sounds.

promptingTabs.label: Cost control
promptingTabs.title: 4K escalation checklist
promptingTabs.description: Use this before moving from Standard or Pro into 4K.
promptingTabs.copy:
Checklist
1. Prompt is approved in a lower-cost Kling route.
2. Source frame or end frame is final.
3. Aspect ratio and duration match delivery.
4. Audio on/off is decided.
5. Only the approved shot moves to Kling 3 4K.

Example:
Approved Kling 3 Pro 1080p shot becomes the 4K master: same subject, same camera path, same timing, same source frame, native 4K output for final delivery.

demoPrompt: Native 4K Kling 3 delivery render, 16:9, 6 seconds, audio off.
A premium smartwatch rests on a dark graphite plinth in a minimal studio.
One slow dolly push reveals the polished edge, glass reflection and screen detail.
Locked product framing, crisp highlights, clean black background, no added text or logo changes.
Use this only after the composition and timing are approved in Standard or Pro.

demoSummary.subject: Premium smartwatch product master
demoSummary.action: Slow reveal of edge, reflection and screen detail
demoSummary.style: Minimal studio, graphite surface, crisp product highlights

custom.tipsIntro: Best practices, cost controls and delivery limits for final native 4K renders with Kling 3 4K.
strengths: Native 4K delivery after the creative direction is approved.
Strong fit for product masters, premium ads and large-screen review files.
Text-to-video and image-to-video workflows with optional audio.
Useful when final framing and detail matter more than iteration cost.
troubleshootingItems: Cost climbs quickly -> validate in Standard or Pro, then render only approved shots in 4K.
Framing drifts -> use a clean source image and specify locked composition.
Audio feels unnecessary -> turn audio off for editorial or music-led finishing.
Motion is too busy -> reduce to one readable action and one camera move.
boundaries: Use text or one source image; no video input on the current MaxVideoAI Kling 3 4K route.
Native 4K is intended for selected delivery shots, not broad prompt exploration.
Clips remain short-form, up to 15 seconds per generation.
1:1, 16:9 and 9:16 are supported aspect ratios on this route.
Use the final quote in Generate before client-facing rollout or automated workflows.
custom.safetyNote: Built-in safeguards and best practices for responsible final delivery with Kling 3 4K.
